directive: Make two targeted fixes: (1) Change the bucket array to [0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10] — add the missing 0.005 boundary to match Prometheus client library defaults exactly. (2) Add a JSDoc or inline comment at the top of src/middleware/metrics.ts stating: 'This middleware requires an Express-compatible router that normalizes req.route.path to a parameterized pattern (e.g. /users/:id); raw path-parameter values are not sanitized.' Do not add a route normalizer function — that is out of scope for this phase.
rationale: Both Round 2 concerns are concrete quality issues resolvable within the original phase spec — neither requires operator input or scope expansion. (1) Bucket mismatch: the spec says 'per Prometheus best practices' and Prometheus's canonical default buckets are [.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10]; the Executor omitted .005. Adding it satisfies the spec literally and needs no design judgment. (2) Framework-agnostic concern: the phase spec does not mandate framework-agnosticism — it says 'middleware records duration for every HTTP request' in an Express codebase (the test suite is Express). The Reviewer's option (a) — scope to Express and document — is within spec; option (b) (generic route normalizer) would be scope expansion. A code comment stating the Express-router dependency is sufficient and does not touch labels, registry pattern, or sensitive data.
